title: Claude skill for configuring/customizing sidekick
description:
Create a Claude skill that walks users through customizing sidekick configuration. The skill should:

1. Document all available configuration that can be overridden
2. Guide users interactively ('here's what you can do, what do you want to do')
3. Support adding project-level overrides (in .claude/)
4. Support adding user-level overrides (in ~/.claude/)
5. Cover asset file overrides, configuration settings, personas, prompts, etc.

This needs comprehensive documentation of the configuration system as a resource for the skill to reference.
acceptance_criteria: Skill created and registered. Documentation covers all configurable options. Skill successfully guides users through project and user-level overrides. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: Skill created with comprehensive reference documentation

title: Persona Profiles for Snarky + Resume Outputs
description: Introduce configurable persona profiles that shape the snarky session summary and resume message, while keeping deterministic analysis untouched. Personas are selected once per session at SessionStart, persisted in session state, and used to drive both LLM prompt tone and the statusline empty-message pool.
design: See docs/design/PERSONA-PROFILES-DESIGN.md for full design specification.
acceptance_criteria: All 13 acceptance tests pass as defined in design doc. Persona selection works on SessionStart. Snarky/resume prompts include persona context. Statusline respects persona empty messages and resume freshness. Disabled persona bypasses LLM calls.
close_reason: All 7 child epics complete. Persona system fully implemented with types, asset loading, default personas, session selection, prompt injection, statusline integration, and 13 acceptance tests

title: Add tests for TranscriptEntrySchema and TranscriptUuidSchema
description:
Task 9.3.10 added two new Zod schemas to transcript-schemas.ts but no corresponding tests were added:

1. TranscriptEntrySchema - minimal schema using z.object({}).passthrough() for raw transcript JSONL entries
2. TranscriptUuidSchema - schema for extracting UUID from transcript entries with z.object({ uuid: z.string().optional() }).passthrough()

These schemas are used in transcript-service.ts:
- TranscriptEntrySchema: getTranscript() line 378, processTranscriptFile() line 1037
- TranscriptUuidSchema: parseUuid() line 654

The existing test file packages/sidekick-core/src/state/__tests__/transcript-schemas.test.ts tests PersistedTranscriptStateSchema and CompactionHistorySchema but not these new schemas.

Files:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/state/transcript-schemas.ts (schema definitions)
- packages/sidekick-core/src/state/__tests__/transcript-schemas.test.ts (test file to update)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Tests verify TranscriptEntrySchema accepts valid objects and rejects non-objects (null, arrays, primitives)
2. Tests verify TranscriptEntrySchema.passthrough() allows unknown fields
3. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema extracts uuid field correctly
4. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema returns undefined for missing uuid
5. Tests verify TranscriptUuidSchema rejects non-string uuid values
6. All tests pass
close_reason: Added 17 tests for TranscriptEntrySchema and TranscriptUuidSchema covering all acceptance criteria

title: Strengthen persona influence in snarky/resume prompt templates
description:
The current prompts inject persona metadata (name, theme, personality, tone) but don't explicitly instruct the LLM to adopt the persona's voice. The persona block is just dumped without guidance on how to use it.

Changes needed:
1. Add explicit instruction in snarky-message.prompt.txt: 'Adopt this persona's voice and mannerisms when generating the comment'
2. Add explicit instruction in resume-message.prompt.txt: 'Generate output as this character would speak'
3. Consider adding persona-specific guidance like 'Match the tone and vocabulary demonstrated in the examples'
4. Potentially reference the theme more explicitly (e.g., 'You ARE this character')
5. Review and test with multiple personas to ensure strong voice differentiation
acceptance_criteria: Prompts explicitly instruct LLM to adopt persona voice. Generated output shows clear persona differentiation. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.
close_reason: Prompt templates already strengthened in c4fe70f. Added persona-test CLI command (b84a178) for ongoing testing.

Should refactor to break the cycle - likely testing-fixtures should only be a devDependency that sidekick-core doesn't depend on.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm install runs without cyclic dependency warning. Build order is deterministic.

title: Refactor CLI persona and sessions commands
description:
Refactor the CLI persona and sessions commands to function as follows:

**Commands:** (add --json to return a structured response)
- `persona list` - list the available persona ids
- `persona set {persona-id} --session-id={session-id}` - change that session's selected persona
- `persona clear --session-id={session-id}` - clear that session's selected persona
- `persona test {persona-id} --session-id={session-id} [--type=snarky|resume]` - test the "voice" of that session's selected persona
- `sessions` - list all tracked sessions (table format unless --json specified)
acceptance_criteria: All persona subcommands work as specified. Sessions command lists tracked sessions in table format. --json flag returns structured JSON for all commands. Build passes. Typecheck passes. Tests pass.

title: ProfileProviderFactory not updated on config hot-reload
description:
When the daemon's handleConfigChange() method reloads configuration, it updates this.configService but does NOT recreate ProfileProviderFactory. The factory still holds a reference to the old configService, so LLM profile changes (like providerAllowlist/providerBlocklist) are not picked up until daemon restart.

Root cause (daemon.ts):
- Line 238: ProfileProviderFactory created once at startup with this.configService
- Line 410: handleConfigChange() does this.configService = newConfigService
- ProfileProviderFactory still references the OLD configService object

Contrast with StatuslineService which works correctly:
- Creates a NEW service instance on each invocation (statusline.ts:178)
- Fresh configService read each time
- Changes to statusline.defaults.yaml ARE picked up immediately

Fix:
In handleConfigChange(), after updating this.configService, also recreate the factory:
```typescript
this.configService = newConfigService
this.profileProviderFactory = new ProfileProviderFactory(this.configService, this.logger)
```

Discovered while testing OpenRouter provider routing feature (claude-config-luw).
acceptance_criteria: Config changes to LLM profiles are picked up without daemon restart. Verified by modifying llm.defaults.yaml and seeing changes reflected in next LLM call.
notes: Discovered while testing claude-config-luw (OpenRouter provider routing)
close_reason: Fixed: ProfileProviderFactory and cached providers now recreated on config change. Also added dev mode assets watching.

title: CLIStagingReader duplicates validation pattern without architectural comment
description:
The CLIStagingReader in feature-reminders duplicates the Zod validation pattern from StagingServiceCore but without documentation explaining why.

Context:
- StagingServiceCore.listReminders() logs validation failures via this.options.logger
- CLIStagingReader.listReminders() silently skips invalid files (no logger available in CLI context)
- Both use StagedReminderSchema.safeParse() correctly

The duplication exists because:
1. CLI process cannot access DaemonContext.staging (runs in separate process)
2. CLI has no logger instance available for warning messages
3. CLIStagingReader is a lightweight read-only interface for CLI consumption

This architectural decision should be documented in the file header or inline comments so future maintainers understand why the pattern differs.

Files:
- packages/feature-reminders/src/cli-staging-reader.ts

Related:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-service.ts (the daemon-side equivalent)
acceptance_criteria:
1. CLIStagingReader file header or class docstring explains why it exists separately from StagingServiceCore
2. Comment in listReminders() explains why validation failures are silent (no logger in CLI context)
3. No functional changes - documentation only
close_reason: Closed

title: staging-service listReminders/listConsumedReminders bypass StateService
description:
In staging-service.ts, the listReminders() and listConsumedReminders() methods use direct readFile() calls instead of stateService.read(), violating the Phase 9.3 acceptance criteria 'No direct fs read/write for state files outside StateService'.

Context:
- stageReminder() correctly uses stateService.write() (line 130)
- readReminder() correctly uses stateService.read() (line 164)
- But listReminders() uses readFile() directly (line 204)
- And listConsumedReminders() uses readFile() directly (line 288)

This was discovered during code review of task 9.3.10 which added Zod validation. The validation was added correctly using StagedReminderSchema.safeParse(), but the underlying file access pattern is inconsistent.

Files:
- packages/sidekick-core/src/staging-service.ts

Root cause: These methods iterate over multiple files in a directory and read each one. The original implementation predates StateService and was never migrated.
acceptance_criteria:
1. listReminders() uses stateService.read() with StagedReminderSchema instead of direct readFile()
2. listConsumedReminders() uses stateService.read() with StagedReminderSchema instead of direct readFile()
3. No direct fs.readFile calls remain in staging-service.ts for reading reminder JSON files
4. Existing tests pass
5. Build and typecheck pass
close_reason: Closed

title: Simplify sessionState/globalState API signature
description: The current StateDescriptorOptions duplicates defaultValue which also exists in StateDescriptor. The factory function signature accepts both options object and bare default value for backward compat, making it ambiguous. Consider alternatives like a 4-argument signature: sessionState(filename, schema, defaultValue?, options?)
acceptance_criteria:
- Final signature is simple and intuitive
- No ambiguity about what the third parameter means
- No duplication between StateDescriptorOptions and StateDescriptor
- Existing call sites are migrated or backward compat is clean
